User.title: User
User.description: Represents a user account for the ProdCost Pro application.

id.description: Unique identifier for the User entity.

definition.description: Stores user profile information. All user-related data is scoped under this path for clear ownership and security.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier for the user.

definition.description: Collection of admin users. Existence in this collection grants admin privileges.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier for the user with admin privileges.

definition.description: Stores raw material inventory. Only admins can create, read, update, or delete inventory items.

params.name: inventoryId
params.description: The unique identifier for the inventory item.

definition.description: Stores product recipes defined by each user. Includes denormalized 'userId' for authorization independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier for the user (owner of the recipe).

params.name: recipeId
params.description: The unique identifier for the recipe.

definition.description: Stores labor costs associated with each product. Includes denormalized 'userId' for authorization independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier for the user.

params.name: laborCostId
params.description: The unique identifier for the labor cost entry.

definition.description: Stores overhead costs. Includes denormalized 'userId' for authorization independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier for the user.

params.name: overheadCostId
params.description: The unique identifier for the overhead cost entry.

definition.description: Stores consolidated cost data. Includes denormalized 'userId' for authorization independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier for the user.

params.name: consolidationId
params.description: The unique identifier for the cost consolidation entry.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore structure is designed to manage product costing for a bakery application, focusing on inventory, recipes, labor, overhead, and consolidated costs. The structure is optimized for security, scalability, and debuggability following the core design principles. Specifically, it emphasizes Authorization Independence by avoiding hierarchical `get()` calls and uses denormalization to ensure security rules are straightforward and efficient.

To achieve Authorization Independence, ownership is explicitly defined and managed at each level. All user-specific data is stored under the `/users/{userId}` path to ensure that security rules can be based on `request.auth.uid` and path-based ownership. This eliminates the need for complex authorization logic that relies on fetching data from parent documents.

For data collections not directly owned by users (e.g., `inventory`), a membership map is not explicitly required as only admins should be able to modify/read this information, controlled via a DBAC approach using the `roles_admin` collection.

This approach ensures that list operations (QAPs) are secure. For example, listing inventory items doesn't require complex filtering in rules, as the rules simply verify that the request is made by an admin. Similarly, recipe listing is secured by path-based ownership (`/users/{userId}/recipes/{recipeId}`) with denormalized ownership data if sharing features are added in the future.

The structure also supports the integrity of ownership, timestamps, and denormalized data by enforcing strict naming conventions and explicit state modeling.
